reason: The only residual finding is a minor hints gap: "ThreadContextHandler_main" and "ThreadContextHandler_request" are missing from s3 hints, replaced by the generic "ThreadContextHandler". Since the full content of s3 correctly lists both specific variants at their exact queue positions (5 and 13) and in every flow step, retrieval via other strong hints (handler names, flow scenario names) will still surface s3. The generic "ThreadContextHandler" hint also provides partial coverage. The risk is reduced retrieval precision only when a user queries specifically by the variant suffix names, which is an edge case. No content accuracy or completeness issue exists.

findings_assessment.justification: The generic 'ThreadContextHandler' hint provides partial coverage. The s3 section content is complete and accurate — both 'ThreadContextHandler_main' (queue pos 5) and 'ThreadContextHandler_request' (queue pos 13) appear correctly throughout the handler queue and all relevant flow steps. The only degradation is reduced recall precision when a user queries specifically by the variant names. Phase E did not resolve this because the fix agent returned the file unchanged — the hints array in the Phase E output is identical to Phase D output, indicating the edit was not applied.
